reviewer: runtime-stage-reviewer (readonly subagent; artifact persisted by orchestrator)

optimizationReadiness.reason: Failure cluster is stable and reproducible: 6/18 traces exit with zero grounded tool calls on answerable named-target prompts, all carrying decisionReasonCode=contract_scope_ambiguity. Fixing the deterministic named-target resolution path is the highest-leverage, least-risky Layer 3 experiment.

Dominant failure cluster `named_target_without_resolution` has traceCount=6 (q2,q8,q9,q10,q12,q17). Each of these prompts is marked answerable and the recommended grounded method was already surfaced in the clarification menu, so the runtime had everything it needed to execute. This is a mechanical failure in the deterministic named-target resolution path, not a noisy LLM artifact.

Paired with toolSelection: the clarification gate is firing because named-target resolution upstream did not bind a concrete market. Do NOT optimize clarification in isolation; it will fix itself once toolSelection is unblocked.

Execution is a downstream symptom. The analyzer reports a calibration artifact (uniform execution=2 for all successful grounded answers). After correction, execution mean is ~3.33 driven by the same upstream toolSelection failures (traces that never called any tool).
Synthesis is downstream of toolSelection. The five low-synthesis traces (q2,q8,q9,q10,q12) have no grounded evidence to synthesize because toolSelection exited early. Do not mean-chase.
Cross-cutting symptom of the same upstream toolSelection failure; fix the root cause first.
Above automation floor; secondary cluster `canonical_dataset_truncation` (traceCount 5) affected q1,q6,q7,q11,q18 but all still produced grounded answers. Not a Layer 3 blocker.

dominantFailureClusters.label: named_target_without_resolution
dominantFailureClusters.description: The runtime returned clarification_required with zero grounded tool calls on an answerable named-target prompt. The clarification menu already listed the correct recommended grounded method; the deterministic named-target resolution path failed to bind the recommendation and execute it.

dominantFailureClusters.label: canonical_dataset_truncation
dominantFailureClusters.description: Grounded answer returned, but canonical dataset was truncated by context-window pressure. Secondary concern; above automation floor.

interactionRisks: toolSelection ↔ clarification: paired fix; fixing deterministic named-target resolution should lift both.
toolSelection ↔ synthesis / synthesisBudget: synthesis and synthesisBudget means will lift automatically when toolSelection is unblocked; do not mean-chase them with a separate experiment.
calibrationNotes: analyze-stage-traces.mjs assigns a uniform execution=2 to all successful iterative-execution traces (miscalibration). Real execution quality is ~3.33 once successful grounded answers are scored on their own evidence. This affects the automation floor check for `execution` but NOT the selection of toolSelection as the Layer 3 target.
paredFixHypothesisNotes: The hypothesis generator should propose a contributor-agnostic change to the deterministic named-target resolution path (in context/lib/ai/) so that when a clarification menu already recommends exactly one grounded method for an answerable named-target prompt, the runtime binds it and executes rather than returning clarification_required.
